about.title: About
paragraphs: I am an R&D Software Engineer at Siemens EDA, developing scalable simulation technologies for Solido FastSPICE.
My work focuses on enabling accurate and efficient circuit simulation for large-scale designs.
Prior to Siemens EDA, I worked on digital power analysis software development.
During my master's studies at KAIST, I conducted research on power integrity, machine learning-based optimization, and advanced design methodologies.
Beyond EDA, I enjoy studying semiconductor devices, circuit theory, numerical analysis, computer architecture, and the emerging applications of AI and large language models.

items.school: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)
items.description: Research in Power Integrity, Machine Learning-based Prediction/Optimization, Circuit Simulation, and Electronic Design Automation (Design Methodology).

items.degree: B.S. in Electronics Engineering (Summa Cum Laude)

highlights: Developing scalable simulation technologies for Solido FastSPICE.
Researching circuit partitioning and signal modeling techniques.
Investigating model order reduction (MOR) and numerical algorithms.
Developed methodologies for digital power estimation and simulation.

items.title: On-Chip Decoupling Capacitor Placement with Impedance Constraint for DRAM Design
items.venue: Proc. Int’l Symp. on Machine Learning for CAD (MLCAD)
items.summary: Machine learning-based optimization framework for DRAM power delivery networks and decoupling capacitor placement.

items.title: Fast and Accurate Analysis of Power Distribution Network Impedance for DRAM Design
items.authors: Minseung Shin, Shilong Zhang, and Youngsoo Shin
items.venue: Proc. Int’l Symp. on Circuits and Systems (ISCAS),
items.summary: Deep learning-based impedance prediction methodology for rapid PDN analysis.

items.title: Compact Q-Learning-Based Standard Cell Layout Compiler for 3nm GAAFET and Beyond
items.authors: Minseung Shin, Jongbeom Kim, Yunjeong Shin, and Taigon Song
items.summary: Q-learning-based standard cell layout optimization framework for advanced technology nodes.

items.title: High-throughput PIM (Processing in-Memory) for DRAM using Bank-level Pipelined Architecture,
items.authors: Hyunsoo Lee, Hyundong Lee, Minseung Shin, Gyuri Shin, Sumin Jeon, and Taigon Song
items.summary: High-throughput processing in-memory architecture for DRAM applications.
